channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean roads in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern section of unused roads that once linked it with the South, with the rivals exchanging threats days after Pyongyang’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its capital.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China unveils its plans to turn its feats in space exploration into scientific advances
item.description: China has made great strides in exploring space in recent years, rocketing astronauts to its own space station and bringing back rocks from the moon.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council expressed “strong concern” Monday as Israel has fired on and wounded U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon during intensified fighting, reiterating its support for their role in supporting security in the region. It's the first statement by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez criticizes Biden administration over 'unrestrained' Israel government
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) criticized the Biden administration on Monday over what she called an “unrestrained” Israeli government.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Why US is sending troops, prized air defense system to Israel
item.description: The U.S. announced this week a major new air defense system for Israel, along with the deployment of 100 American troops to operate it in the country, in a development that will bolster Israeli security but potentially put American forces at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Pandas en route to National Zoo after a year
item.description: Two new giant pandas are reportedly en route to the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. “Something *giant* is coming to Washington, D.C. via the @FedEx Panda Express!” the zoo said in a post on social media platform X on Monday. “The Zoo will be closed to the public tomorrow, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Poland's leader defends his decision to suspend the right to asylum
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk on Monday defended a plan to suspend the right to asylum as human rights and civil society organizations argued that fundamental rights must be respected.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vial that contained nerve agent that killed UK woman contained enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The lead counsel for a public inquiry into the 2018 death of a British woman poisoned by a Soviet-developed nerve agent said Monday that there was enough poison in the vial she unwittingly opened to kill thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces court on charges of embezzling EU funds
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen strongly denied committing any wrongdoing at a Paris court Monday as she and her National Rally party stand trial over the suspected embezzlement of European Parliament funds.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India-Canada diplomatic war intensifies over killing probe
item.description: India has strongly rejected Canada's allegations that its diplomats are "persons of interest" in a murder investigation, stating that the claims are part of Trudeau's "political agenda" and that Canada had failed to provide evidence to support its claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: EU condemns attacks on peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: The European Union on Monday condemned attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon and rejected Israeli allegations that the U.N. was keeping them there to obstruct military operations against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 50 well-preserved Viking Age skeletons unearthed in Denmark
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark have unearthed a Viking Age burial ground containing some 50 “exceptionally well-preserved” skeletons.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Udine on high alert ahead of Italy's match against Israel
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The city of Udine is on high alert as Italy prepares to host Israel in a competitive soccer match on Monday.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu mulls plan to empty northern Gaza of civilians and cut off aid to those left inside
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is examining a plan to seal off humanitarian aid to northern Gaza in an attempt to starve out Hamas militants, a plan that, if implemented, could trap without food or water hundreds of thousands of Palestinians unwilling or unable to leave t
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden-Harris admin accused of ‘too little, too late’ to save the people of war-torn, famine-stricken Sudan
item.description: Critics say the Biden administration is in danger of waiting too long to stop the killing and help the people of Sudan get humanitarian aid, as it makes a last-minute effort to make a difference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ailing and silenced in prison, Belarus activist symbolizes the nation's repression
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — The last time any of Maria Kolesnikova's family had contact with the imprisoned Belarusian opposition activist was more than 18 months ago. Fellow inmates at the penal colony reported hearing her plead for medical help from inside her tiny and smelly cell.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Interpol launches campaign to help solve 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were found in Europe
item.description: Interpol launched an initiative last week to seek the public's help in solving 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were discovered in Europe between 1982 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Macao's former top judge is chosen as the Chinese casino hub's first leader born in mainland China
item.description: MACAO (AP) — Macao’s former top judge, Sam Hou Fai, was chosen as the Chinese casino hub’ s next leader in a largely ceremonial election on Sunday, setting him up to become the city’s first chief executive born in mainland China.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says front-line units ready to strike South Korea if more drones appear
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Sunday its front-line army units are ready to launch strikes on South Korea, ramping up pressure on its rival that it said flew drones and dropped leaflets over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon will send advanced missile defense system, military personnel to Israel
item.description: The U.S. will send to Israel a missile defense system and military personnel to operate it, the Pentagon announced Sunday. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin authorized the deployment of a Terminal High-Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery "and associated crew of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Biden: Israel should 'absolutely' stop strikes on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: President Biden said Friday that Israel should “absolutely” stop strikes on the U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon, following two incidents the group was involved in this week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: The air raid on central Beirut — the deadliest in over a year of war — apparently targeted two residential buildings in separate neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: BILAL HUSSEIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
